projectDescription: Reinforcement learning agents that play Connect 4 using Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) and Q-Learning, with support for head-to-head agent evaluation and self-play training.
keyFeatures: Monte Carlo Tree Search agent with UCB1-guided selection and random playout simulation
Q-Learning agent with epsilon-greedy exploration and mirror-state symmetry optimization
Configurable board dimensions (rows × columns) for both agents
MCTS vs MCTS head-to-head evaluation with configurable playout counts
MCTS vs Q-Learning evaluation using pretrained Q-value tables
Q-Learning training loop against MCTS opponent with reward shaping
Persistent Q-value storage via gzip-compressed pickle files
Moving average reward plotting with matplotlib
Random baseline player for simulations and benchmarking
publicApiSurface: Q_Learning(player, alpha, discount_factor, epsilon, r, c)
main() — CLI entry point: choose MCTS vs MCTS or MCTS vs Q-Learning
MCTS_vs_MCTS(x, y) — run MCTS agent matchup
MCTS_vs_Q() — evaluate pretrained Q-Learning agent against MCTS
train_qlearning() — train Q-Learning agent against MCTS with 50,000 episodes
